summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture of the legendary 8/22/93 Eugene shows, highlighted by a smoking Help > Slip > Franklin’s and an epic Jack Straw jam.
review: The SBD recording is exceptionally clean – crisp highs, solid mids and a surprisingly tight low end for a 1993 tape.  Musically the two‑night run is celebrated for Jerry’s inspired playing (despite mixed feelings about his new “Lightning Bolt” guitar), an unforgettable Help/Slip/Franklin’s sequence, a blistering Jack Straw jam and standout renditions of Broken Arrow, Music Never Stopped and Days Between the Lines.

ai_rating.rationale: The vast majority of reviews are 4‑5 stars praising both performance and audio quality; only a handful of low or critical notes exist, giving an overall weighted rating around 4.4.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Listeners repeatedly note crystal‑clear mix, superb balance and “studio‑like” clarity; a few mention slightly thin bass on some systems but no major dropouts. One reviewer flagged a broken file, otherwise the source is considered top‑shelf.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set builds steadily, highlighted by a fiery Jack Straw that segues into an extended jam; second set opens with a legendary Help/Slip/Franklin’s and rides a wave of energy through Broken Arrow, Music Never Stopped and Days Between, delivering a cohesive, high‑energy arc.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for “ballistic” playing and pure tone, though his new Lightning Bolt guitar divides opinion – some love the acoustic‑ish bite, others find it flat.
band_member_comments.Phil: Featured on the banner “Let Phil Sing”; his vocal work on Broken Arrow is called flawless by several reviewers, with a single note of strain mentioned.
band_member_comments.Bob: Infamous for leaving stage during Jack Straw due to guitar problems; fans love the drama and the way the band carried on, noting his rhythm chops when he returned.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince (keyboards) described as “lost but keeping chords” in some reviews; synth tones are noted as being lower in the mix – a welcome change for some listeners.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill and Mickey receive praise for tight, energetic playing; one reviewer calls Mickey’s drumming “electric” and highlights the power of the drum‑solo sections.
